channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Congress looks to ban Chinese drones widely used in US: What to know about the debate
item.description: Lawmakers in Washington are seeking to ban new sales of drones from two dominant Chinese manufacturers, arguing they could be used to spy on Americans and that the low-cost models are hurting the U.S. drone industry.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gaetz Ethics report finds ‘substantial evidence’ of statutory rape, drug use
item.description: The House Ethics Committee found “substantial evidence” that former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) paid a 17-year-old high school student for sex when he was 35, used illicit drugs like cocaine and ecstasy, and obstructed Congress’s investigation into his conduct.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New York City police apprehend suspect in the death of a woman found on fire in a subway car
item.description: New York City police announced Sunday they have in custody a “person of interest” in the early morning death of a woman who they believe may have fallen asleep on a stationary subway train before being intentionally lit on fire by a man she didn't know.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump threatens to try to take back the Panama Canal. Panama's president balks at the suggestion
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Donald Trump suggested Sunday that his new administration could try to regain control of the Panama Canal that the United States “foolishly” ceded to its Central American ally, contending that shippers are charged “ridiculous” fees to pass through the vital transportation channel link
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden commutes sentences for nearly all federal death row inmates and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luigi Mangione appears in Manhattan court to face state charges in death of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of fatally shooting the CEO of UnitedHealthcare is expected to be arraigned in a Manhattan court on state murder and terror charges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Violent Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua terrorizing Americans in at least 19 states
item.description: Tren de Aragua has a presence in at least 19 states as of December, which is up from 16 in November, indicating the gang's expansion within the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elderly U.S. Congresswoman 'missing' for months still on taxpayers' payroll: report
item.description: An 81-year-old GOP Congresswoman from Texas, who hasn’t voted on the House floor since July and was recently found to be living in a senior care facility is expected to continue drawing a salary into the new year.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: Former Israeli spies describe attack using exploding electronic devices against Hezbollah
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Two recently retired senior Israeli intelligence agents shared new details about a deadly clandestine operation years in the making that targeted Hezbollah militants in Lebanon and Syria using exploding pagers and walkie talkies three months ago.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What are 'snow rollers'? Photos of rare phenomenon in Michigan spark curiosity
item.description: “This is the coolest thing I've ever seen,” one viewer wrote, remarking on the dozens of doughnut-shaped snowballs peppered across a field.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tiger's son makes the ace. Bernhard Langer makes the winning putt in playoff over Woods at PNC
item.description: Tiger Woods never had so much fun losing in a playoff. For the 67-year-old Bernhard Langer, it never gets old winning the PNC Championship with his son.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Driver killed by police after driving truck through Texas mall, injuring 5
item.description: Authorities said the driver traveled "several hundred yards" through a JCPenney, injuring multiple people.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Jones, ABC 4

item.title: NORAD's Santa tracker was a Cold War morale boost. Now it attracts millions of kids
item.description: The Christmas tradition has become nearly global in scope: Children from around the world track Santa Claus as he sweeps across the earth, delivering presents and defying time.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Virginia woman faces jail time after practicing illegal dentistry
item.description: A Virginia woman has been convicted of practicing dentistry without a license, following accusations that she illegally performed veneer procedures at her tooth jewelry business.
item.dc:creator: Karina F. Garcia, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Missouri company creates healthy dog treats using invasive fish
item.description: A Missouri has found a way to turn an invasive fish into multiple useful products, including a new line of dog treats.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, KRON4

item.title: Tiger Woods and son Charlie share the lead at PNC Championship in Orlando
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Tiger Woods and 15-year-old son Charlie ran off five straight birdies on the back nine Saturday for a 13-under 59 in the scramble format, giving them a share of the lead in the PNC Championship in Woods' first competition since back surgery in September.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida driver hits student with her truck, covers license plate before fleeing, deputies say
item.description: A Florida woman was arrested after deputies say she struck a teenager who was crossing the road with her truck, covered her license plate and fled the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lara Trump says she will not seek to replace Rubio in Senate
item.description: The incoming president announced Rubio as his Secretary of State choice in November. Earlier this month, Lara Trump stepped down as RNC co-chair to “heavily consider” taking on the role in Congress's upper chamber.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Over 600,000 Nuna Rava car seats recalled over possible crash failure
item.description: Nuna Baby Essentials is recalling more than 600,000 of its Rava line of car seats over a harness defect that could cause the restraint system to fail in a crash.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: FDA: Contaminated cucumbers linked to 100 salmonella cases; Recall tied to Missouri and Illinois
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration reports that contaminated cucumbers are linked to 100 salmonella cases across 23 states, following a recall that included products sold in Missouri and Illinois.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, NewsNation

item.title: Trump backs MAGA loyalist Joe Gruters for RNC treasurer
item.description: After the 2021 Capitol siege, Gruters, a former chair for the state GOP, was one of 17 state lawmakers who voted to contest President-elect Joe Biden’s Electoral College win.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Albania to close TikTok for a year blaming it for promoting violence among children
item.description: Albanian authorities held 1,300 meetings with teachers and parents following the stabbing death of a teenager in mid-November by another teen after a quarrel that started on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Llazar Semini, KRON4

item.title: Trump taps 'Apprentice' producer, Mark Burnett, as special envoy to the United Kingdom
item.description: Mark Burnett, the power producer who helped reintroduce Donald Trump to a national television audience with “The Apprentice,” is being tapped by the president-elect as special envoy to the United Kingdom in his upcoming administration.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 Party City stores will stay open despite national closures — here's where
item.description: Party City stores in Hawaii will remain open, even as the national chain announced plans to close 850 locations across the country.
item.dc:creator: Lucy Lopez, WGN 9

item.title: Deal spares federal workers before Christmas but there's concern about possible job cuts under Trump
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — Johnny Zuagar said he tried to hide his worries about a potential government shutdown from his three boys as he weighed how much to spend on Christmas presents. “I’ve got to keep a poker face,” Zuagar, a statistician at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rickey Henderson, MLB's all-time stolen base leader, dies at 65: reports
item.description: Rickey Henderson, the man who changed Major League Baseball with his blazing, unbeatable speed, has died, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KRON4

item.title: What NYC Mayor Adams told Luigi Mangione when they met in NYC
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams said he “wanted to send a strong message” when he met accused UnitedHealthcare CEO killer Luigi Mangione in New York City on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, ABC 4

item.title: NBA fines Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla $35,000 for postgame comments toward officials
item.description: The NBA fined the Boston Celtics coach $35,000 on Saturday “for aggressively pursuing and directing inappropriate language toward a game official” at the end of his team's loss to the Chicago Bulls on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Here's a look at the $100 billion in disaster relief in the government spending bill
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Congress is allocating more than $100 billion in emergency aid designed to address extensive damage caused by disasters after this week's scramble to find consensus on a government spending bill.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate passes RFK Stadium land bill, giving the Washington Commanders a major off-the-field win
item.description: The path for the NFL's Washington Commanders to return to the nation’s capital is clear after an on-again, off-again saga in Congress ended early Saturday with a postmidnight reprieve.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Missouri governor commutes sentence of white police officer convicted of fatally shooting Black man
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — A white former Kansas City police officer who was convicted of involuntary manslaughter in the fatal shooting of a Black man was released from prison Friday after Missouri’s governor commuted his sentence to parole. The decision by Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Amazon and Starbucks workers are on strike. Trump might have something to do with it
item.description: Amazon delivery drivers and Starbucks baristas are on strike in a handful of U.S. cities as they seek to exert pressure on the two major companies to recognize them as unionized employees or to meet demands for an inaugural labor contract.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Report alleges Coast Guard leaders kept sexual assault investigation secret
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (AP) — Former top Coast Guard officials hid a yearslong investigation into sexual assault and harassment at the service's academy from both Congress and the public after leaders debated the fallout from a potential disclosure, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wisconsin police chief overseeing school shooting probe is named to Seattle job
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Madison Police Chief Shon Barnes was named as the chief of police in Seattle on Friday, five days after he was thrust into the national spotlight because of a shooting at a religious school in Wisconsin that killed a teacher and student.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER and TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Traveling for the holidays? These are the busiest times to fly or drive
item.description: Drivers and airline passengers without reindeer and sleighs better make a dash for it: It's beginning to look like another record for holiday travel in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: David Koenig, WGN 9

item.title: Party City going out of business, starting process 'immediately': report
item.description: Party City, once among the top names in party-supply retailers, is going out of business, according to an announcement from CEO Barry Litwin that was viewed by CNN.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Closures, Social Security checks, furloughs: What a government shutdown might mean
item.description: Congress has until midnight Friday to come up with a way to fund the government or federal agencies will shut down, meaning hundreds of thousands of federal employees could be sent home — or stay on the job without pay — just ahead of the holidays.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, ABC 4
